skill.name: Azure Event Grid SDK for Python
skill.description: Azure Event Grid SDK for Python. Use for publishing events, handling CloudEvents, and event-driven architectures.
skill.summary: Event routing service for building event-driven applications with pub/sub semantics.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation and code examples for the Azure Event Grid SDK for Python. Static analysis scanned 0 files with a risk score of 0/100. No security concerns identified as the skill provides reference documentation without any executable code or runtime behavior.

content.user_title: Publish and Route Events with Azure Event Grid
content.value_statement: Build event-driven applications on Azure with this skill. It guides you through publishing CloudEvents and EventGridEvents using the Azure Event Grid SDK for Python.
actual_capabilities: Generate code for publishing CloudEvents to Event Grid topics
Create EventGridEvent payloads with Azure-native schema
Guide async event publishing for high-throughput scenarios
Explain Event Grid Namespaces and pull delivery models
Provide best practices for event batching and retry handling
limitations: This skill provides documentation and code generation only
Requires Azure subscription and Event Grid topic or namespace
Does not execute code or connect to Azure services directly
Limited to Azure Event Grid for Python SDK

use_cases.title: Event-Driven Microservices
use_cases.description: Build decoupled microservices that communicate through Event Grid

use_cases.title: Real-time Event Publishing
use_cases.description: Publish CloudEvents from Python applications to Azure Event Grid

use_cases.title: Serverless Integration
use_cases.description: Connect Azure Functions and Logic Apps through Event Grid events

prompt_templates.title: Publish a Single CloudEvent
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me publish a CloudEvent to Azure Event Grid when a new order is created. The event should include order_id and amount fields.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Event Publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to publish multiple events efficiently using the Azure Event Grid SDK for Python with batching.

prompt_templates.title: Async Event Publishing Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to set up async event publishing for high-throughput scenarios. Guide me through using the async EventGridPublisherClient.

prompt_templates.title: Event Grid Namespace Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to configure and use Event Grid Namespaces for pull delivery. Include authentication and topic configuration.

output_examples.input: Publish a CloudEvent when an order is created
output_examples.output:
Here is the code to publish a CloudEvent for order creation:

from azure.eventgrid import EventGridPublisherClient, CloudEvent
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

client = EventGridPublisherClient(endpoint, DefaultAzureCredential())

event = CloudEvent(
    type="MyApp.Events.OrderCreated",
    source="/myapp/orders",
    data={"order_id": "12345", "amount": 99.99}
)
client.send(event)

output_examples.input: How do I authenticate with Azure Event Grid?
output_examples.output:
Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication:

1. Install: pip install azure-identity azure-eventgrid
2. Set environment variable: EVENTGRID_TOPIC_ENDPOINT
3. Create client: EventGridPublisherClient(endpoint, DefaultAzureCredential())

DefaultAzureCredential supports managed identity, service principal, and local development authentication.

best_practices: Use CloudEvents format for new applications as it is an industry standard for interoperability
Batch multiple events together when publishing to improve throughput and reduce API calls
Include meaningful subjects in events to enable efficient filtering and routing
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode connection strings or credentials in your code. Use environment variables or managed identity.
Avoid sending events synchronously in high-throughput scenarios. Use the async client instead.
Do not use EventGridEvent schema for new applications unless you require Azure-native features. Prefer CloudEvents.

faq.question: What is the difference between CloudEvent and EventGridEvent?
faq.answer: CloudEvent follows the CNCF CloudEvents specification for interoperability across platforms. EventGridEvent uses Azure's native schema with additional Azure-specific features. For new applications, CloudEvent is recommended.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Event Grid?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports multiple authentication methods: managed identity (recommended for Azure-hosted apps), service principal with client secret, or Azure CLI credentials for local development.

faq.question: Can I publish events asynchronously?
faq.answer: Yes. Use azure.eventgrid.aio.EventGridPublisherClient for async operations. This is recommended for high-throughput scenarios and integrates well with async Python frameworks.

faq.question: What are Event Grid Namespaces?
faq.answer: Event Grid Namespaces provide a pull-based delivery model where clients can retrieve events on demand. This differs from the push-based custom topics. Use namespaces when you need more control over event consumption.

faq.question: How do I handle failed event publishing?
faq.answer: Event Grid has built-in retry policies. For custom retry logic, wrap send calls in try-except blocks and implement exponential backoff. Consider using dead-letter queues for persistent failures.

faq.question: What data formats are supported in event payloads?
faq.answer: Event Grid supports JSON payloads by default. You can set datacontenttype to specify the format. Common types include application/json, application/xml, and text/plain.
